title: culcitium denticulatum
intro: Culcitium denticulatum, also known as toothed culcitium, is a plant species in the family Campanulaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the western Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Culcitium denticulatum include its use in treating bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and prevent the formation of kidney stones. Culcitium denticulatum is typically prepared in the form of a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations can be found in various traditional medicine systems, including Ayurveda and Unani.
